ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for prawn burrito bowl; mapped to the nearest parent class 'seafood dish' (FOODON_00002473) as an approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for prawn burrito bowl; A0CKP (composite crustacean dish) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite seafood bowl dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical restaurant-sized prawn burrito bowl with rice, prawns, salsa, vegetables, and toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Burrito Bowl — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Prawn Burrito Bowl (cr_id G00010838) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated using USDA FoodData Central composite analogy for prawn-rice-salsa bowl (see notes).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed rice-seafood bowl
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Prawn Burrito Bowl. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate component being white rice (GI ~64–72) moderated by substantial protein (10.9 g/100g from prawns), fat (13.7 g/100g), dietary fibre (1.4 g/100g), and salsa/vegetable components, consistent with mixed rice-protein-fat bowl analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (20.06 g/100g × 350 g serving = 70.2 g) / 100 = 38.6 g; however, effective available carbohydrate after fibre deduction (70.2 − 4.9 fibre) = 65.3 g; GL = 55 × 28.0 net carb portion / 100 = 15.4 (using realistic 50 g rice equivalent carbohydrate contribution in a 350 g bowl).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 470 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg/100g threshold with insufficient potassium offset (126 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish in standard portions; if consumed, request reduced seasoning and salsa, and avoid added cheese or sour cream to lower sodium intake below 600 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (20.1 g/100g) with low estimated GI (~55); protein from prawns helps moderate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes may consume this dish in controlled portions (approximately 250–300 g); request brown rice to lower GI and increase fibre, and monitor total carbohydrate intake to remain within the 45–60 g per meal target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated cholesterol (212 mg/100g) and sodium (470 mg/100g); fat at 13.7 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~3.8 g/100g from cheese and sour cream
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 200–250 g, request the dish without cheese and sour cream to reduce saturated fat and dietary cholesterol, and choose grilled rather than fried prawns to minimise added fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetables (bell peppers, tomato in salsa) may trigger inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; sodium 470 mg/100g exceeds RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or modify this dish by requesting salsa without nightshade vegetables and reducing sodium-containing toppings; monitor for joint flare after consumption of nightshade-containing preparations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Burrito Bowl is a Tex-Mex inspired composite dish served in Singapore's casual dining and food-court settings. It consists of a base of steamed white rice topped with sautéed or grilled prawns, fresh or cooked vegetables (typically bell peppers, corn, and black beans), tomato salsa, shredded cheese, and sour cream, seasoned with cumin, chili, and lime. The dish delivers a balanced macronutrient profile per 100 g: 249 kcal energy, 10.9 g protein from prawns, 13.7 g fat (partly from cheese and sour cream), and 20.1 g carbohydrate from rice and beans. Sodium is elevated at 470 mg/100g due to seasoning, salsa, and dairy toppings, making it unsuitable for hypertension without modification. Cholesterol is notable at 212 mg/100g, reflecting the prawn content. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index (~55) moderated by protein and fat content. It is not suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to nightshade ingredients. Clinically, it is best consumed in controlled portions by individuals managing blood glucose, and modifications such as brown rice substitution and reduced toppings improve its suitability for metabolic conditions. The dish is halal-friendly and widely available in Singapore's Western food stalls and casual restaurants.
common_user_queries: Is prawn burrito bowl suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of prawn burrito bowl
Can I eat prawn burrito bowl with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010838) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogy for comparable prawn-rice-dairy composite dish and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology, clearly documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite Tex-Mex prawn bowl are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (G00010838) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an exact match as of 2026. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) estimated using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a comparable shrimp-rice-cheese-sour-cream composite dish (USDA FDC ID reference: mixed seafood rice bowl analogue); this is an adaptation and not an exact match. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database methodology for mixed rice-protein-fat bowls; no direct GI measurement for Prawn Burrito Bowl exists in the published literature as of 2026. All core macros are non-zero and sourced from HPB with confidence 0.95.
